title: hibbertia coriacea subsp. angustifolia
intro: Hibbertia coriacea subsp. angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved Guinea flower, is a subspecies of flowering plant that is native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to provide relief from conditions such as arthritis and skin infections. The health benefits of Hibbertia coriacea subsp. angustifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea or infusion, and also used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
